type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: CO increase in air releases are due to an increase in gas collections efficiency and 18.5% increase in total LFG sent to the flare.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increases in PM10 air releases are due to increase in gas collection efficiency and 18.5% increase in total landfill gas sent to the flare; as well as 40% increase in the amount of material handled and stockpiled.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: PM2.5 air release increases are due to an increase in gas collection efficiency and 18.5% increase in total landfill gas sent to flare; as well as a 40% increase in the amount of material handled and stockpiled.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increased amount in LFG sent to the flare in 2013 (i.e. approximately 260% increase based on 2012 LFG volume).  LFG collection system for the landfill expansion continues to expand and collect more LFG.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The reportable PM2.5 and PM10 releases increased in 2012.  These increases are due to an increased amount of methane sent to on-site landfill flare.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The reportable PM2.5 releases increased.  These increases are due to an increased amount of methane sent to on-site landfill flare.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The reportable PM10 releases decrease in 2011 relative to 2010.  Although there is an increase in PM10 releases from the flare, a significant decrease in erosion releases is experienced in 2011, resulting in an overall decrease if PM10.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The PM2.5 releases increase in 2011 relative to 2010 to above the reporting threshold.  The majority of PM2.5 releases originate from the on-site stationary combustion equipment (landfill gas-fired flares) and an increased amount of methane sent to flare resulted in an increase in PM2.5
